verdict_reasoning: HMGB1 is a canonical high-mobility-group protein and a non-histone, nuclear DNA-binding factor. It localizes to the nucleus where it regulates transcription and DNA organization. While HMGB1 is released extracellularly upon cell damage or active secretion (acting as a DAMP in inflammation), it has no membrane-anchoring mechanism—no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or stable surface tethering—and is not surface-accessible on the living cell per se. The extracellular pool is not anchored to the cell surface via its own mechanism.
